summary:
The patient has locally advanced laryngeal carcinoma involving the anterior commissure and right vocal cord with associated perichondritis, indicating significant local tissue invasion. In such cases, surgical removal—particularly total laryngectomy—is more appropriate than radical radiotherapy alone due to the extent of invasions; thus, first-line management should prioritize definitive surgical excision followed potentially by adjunctive therapies.  

Final answer choice based on rationale above: $\boxed{\text{D}}$
